direct_children.title: WPHX-301 — Bootstrap, load order, constants, environment
direct_children.close_reason: Verified WPHX-301 bootstrap trace scaffold: npm run wp:bootstrap:trace:check passes; normal, SHORTINIT, install, and recovery scenario gates from the typed Haxe fixture match the locked WordPress 7.0 oracle markers, with ordered include/load phase digests recorded for wp-load.php/wp-settings.php and scaffolded ownership captured in manifests/ownership/wphx-301-bootstrap-workset.v1.json. Receipt: receipts/wp-bootstrap/wphx-301-bootstrap-traces.v1.json.

direct_children.title: WPHX-302 — Hooks/plugin API
direct_children.close_reason: Verified WPHX-302 hooks/plugin API surface: npm run wp:hooks:surface:check passes with 56/56 ABI symbols covered for plugin.php and class-wp-hook.php; generated hook shell and copied WordPress 7.0 oracle match for deprecated hooks, ref-array actions, remove-all helpers, plugin path helpers, activation/deactivation hooks, and uninstall hook registration across local PHP plus pinned Docker PHP 8.4/8.5. Ownership is recorded as a bounded temporary bridge in manifests/ownership/wphx-302-hooks-workset.v1.json, with WPHX-303 opened to promote the hook bridge toward a Haxe parity candidate. Receipt: receipts/wp-hooks/wphx-302-hook-surface.v1.json.

direct_children.title: WPHX-303 — Error, deprecation, formatting, escaping, KSES
direct_children.close_reason: Completed WPHX-303 domain slice: surface inventory, escaping/sanitization fixture, WP_Error typed parity candidate, deprecation/native error signaling fixture, KSES fixture, and selected pure formatting Haxe parity candidates all pass local PHP plus pinned Docker PHP runtime checks. Recorded external-oracle and haxe_parity_candidate ownership manifests, parent closure receipt, and WPHX-700.01 follow-up for broader KSES security/block/feed integration coverage.

direct_children.title: WPHX-304 — Options, transients, object cache
direct_children.close_reason: Closed after WPHX-304.01 through WPHX-304.07 completed. Fresh audit passed surface, option-storage, transient, object-cache, serialization/cache, settings/defaults, option-cache candidate, Haxe escape-hatch, Beads graph, and receipt validation checks. Remaining non-closure gaps are now tracked by WPHX-305 (wpdb/database abstraction), WPHX-311 (REST settings/schema), WPHX-317 (multisite/network site-option and site-transient paths), and WPHX-322 (PHP first-party manifest/upstream PHPUnit closure).

direct_children.title: WPHX-305 — wpdb and database abstraction
direct_children.close_reason: Completed WPHX-305 wpdb/database abstraction workset: surface inventory, deterministic fixtures, live MySQL/MariaDB gates, typed Haxe candidate slices through prepare/query/db_connect/public-state/native mysqli boundaries, locked PHP DB-client runtime matrix, manifests, ownership records, and receipts are in place.

direct_children.title: WPHX-306 — Users, roles, capabilities, auth, cookies, nonces
direct_children.close_reason: Closed WPHX-306 users/auth domain with a bounded domain-closure receipt. The domain now has a surface inventory, typed Haxe AuthAdapterContract candidate, capability/role fixture, auth-cookie/nonce fixture, password/application-password fixture, runtime ABI fixture, installed-style HTTP auth gate, evidence links, and progress-matrix update. This is not a generated public PHP replacement claim: public auth files remain oracle-source package fixtures, and full MySQL-backed installed WordPress auth/admin/plugin/upstream-PHPUnit closure remains WPHX-700 or later distribution work.

direct_children.title: WPHX-307 — Posts, metadata, revisions, WP_Query
direct_children.close_reason: Closed WPHX-307 posts/metadata/revisions/WP_Query source-domain slice with receipts for surface inventory, typed Haxe adapter-contract candidates, WP_Query runtime/query-state, live DB SQL/result matrix, provisioned upstream PHPUnit pass/pass groups, and installed-distribution posts/query HTTP behavior. Closure remains bounded: generated public PHP replacement and full database-backed installed post behavior are deferred.

direct_children.title: WPHX-308 — Taxonomy, terms, comments
direct_children.close_reason: Closed bounded WPHX-308 taxonomy/terms/comments domain evidence slice across surface inventory, Haxe adapter-contract, CRUD/count/cache/query/live-DB fixtures, selected upstream PHPUnit pass/pass evidence, installed-distribution gate, progress matrix, and domain closure receipt.

direct_children.title: WPHX-310 — Themes, theme JSON, template hierarchy

direct_children.title: WPHX-322 — PHP first-party manifest closure
direct_children.close_reason: Added deterministic WPHX-322 first-party PHP manifest closure/split runner and receipt. The manifest assigns 1,564 C1 WordPress 7.0 PHP runtime source entries and 1,222 C1 PHP distribution artifacts to closed receipt-backed domains or explicit WPHX-306 through WPHX-320 follow-up owners, with zero unassigned; PHP vendor entries are excluded to WPHX-323. This is manifest ownership/split closure, not a broad Haxe implementation claim.

direct_children.title: WPHX-323 — PHP vendor manifest closure
direct_children.close_reason: Closed by WPHX-323 PHP vendor manifest closure. Added deterministic generator/check for manifests/wp-core/wphx-323-php-vendor-manifest-closure.v1.json, ownership manifest, and receipt; verified 293 PHP vendor_source entries, 293 matching distribution artifacts, 16 preserved vendor/library boundaries, and the 6 WPHX-312 preserved-vendor copied-surface gates. Filed follow-up wordpresshx-l76.27 / WPHX-323.01 for future Haxe-owned vendor replacement planning.

validation_result.parent_status_policy: validated while WPHX-300 is in_progress or closed; direct child closure and evidence receipts are the stable invariants
remaining_gaps_preserved: WPHX-300 closes the bounded PHP-core evidence workset, not complete Haxe ownership of all WordPress PHP runtime logic.
Generated public PHP replacement for all first-party WordPress original paths remains incomplete and requires WPHX PHP generated-overlay/original-path adapter evidence before stronger claims.

Many domain closures intentionally preserve copied-oracle fixtures, bridge routers, adapter-contract candidates, selected upstream ratchets, or blocked installed/e2e gate declarations rather than installed distribution parity.

Full installed WordPress behavior across database-backed state, browser/admin/editor flows, plugin/theme ecosystem compatibility, filesystem/network/mail side effects, and production-like runtimes remains WPHX-700 or later distribution work.
Preserved PHP vendor and bundled-library boundaries remain governed by WPHX-323 replacement/provenance decisions; copied artifact retirement is not claimed by this parent closure.
Browser/Gutenberg package ownership, editor UI behavior, visual/performance parity, and asset pipeline ownership remain WPHX-400/WPHX-500/WPHX-700 work.
This receipt does not change the super-progress score; it reconciles Beads parent status with already-recorded child receipts and non-claims.

non_claims: WPHX-300 closes the bounded PHP-core evidence workset, not complete Haxe ownership of all WordPress PHP runtime logic.

Browser/Gutenberg package ownership, editor UI behavior, visual/performance parity, and asset pipeline ownership remain WPHX-400/WPHX-500/WPHX-700 work.
